revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵== See Also ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''.== See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "== See Also! ==" to "== See Also =="
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.comment: removing duplicates of this
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡}}

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.

The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.

These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|12=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|12=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|12=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|12=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|12=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|12=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.text:
[[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|大室公園整備前の大室古墳群
{{国土航空写真}}（1986年度撮影）。]] '''大室古墳群'''（おおむろこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[東大室町]]・[[西大室町]]にある[[古墳群]]。4基が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 [[赤城山]]の南麓の城南地区に分布しており、[[前二子古墳]]・[[中二子古墳]]・[[後二子古墳]]の3基の大型[[前方後円墳]]を中心に築造されている。築造年代は、[[6世紀]]初頭から6世紀後半にかけてであり、前二子山古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳の順に造営されたと推定されている。 == 主な古墳 == * [[前二子古墳]] * [[中二子古墳]] * [[後二子古墳]] * [[小二子古墳]] * 内堀1号墳(M1号墳) * 内堀4号墳(M4号墳) この他、内堀1号墳の西側などにも小円墳群が存在していたことが発掘により判明している。 File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|前二子古墳 File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|中二子古墳 File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|後二子古墳 File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|小二子古墳 File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1号墳 File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4号墳 == 大室公園 == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|大室公園・時の広場]] {{Main|大室公園}} 現在、大室古墳群の周辺は[[公園]]として整備されており、[[日本の歴史公園100選]]に選定されている。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 前二子古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|527|前二子古墳}}。 * 中二子古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|528|中二子古墳}}。 * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *前橋市教育委員会(編)　『前橋市 大室古墳群－前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳－』 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[ヤマト王権]] * [[毛野]] * [[梅木遺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 国指定文化財等データベース 前二子古墳] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 中二子古墳] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 文化遺産データベース 前二子古墳] - [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 中二子古墳] - [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 大室古墳群] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html 前橋市 大室古墳群について] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html 大室公園] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=群馬県}} {{デフォルトソート:おおむろこふんくんくんまけん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
[[File:Omuro-kofungun (Gunma) 1986.jpg|thumb|大室公園整備前の大室古墳群
{{国土航空写真}}（1986年度撮影）。]] '''大室古墳群'''（おおむろこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[東大室町]]・[[西大室町]]にある[[古墳群]]。4基が国の[[史跡]]に指定されている。 [[赤城山]]の南麓の城南地区に分布しており、[[前二子古墳]]・[[中二子古墳]]・[[後二子古墳]]の3基の大型[[前方後円墳]]を中心に築造されている。築造年代は、[[6世紀]]初頭から6世紀後半にかけてであり、前二子山古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳の順に造営されたと推定されている。 == 主な古墳 == * [[前二子古墳]] * [[中二子古墳]] * [[後二子古墳]] * [[小二子古墳]] * 内堀1号墳(M1号墳) * 内堀4号墳(M4号墳) この他、内堀1号墳の西側などにも小円墳群が存在していたことが発掘により判明している。 File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|前二子古墳 File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|中二子古墳 File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|後二子古墳 File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|小二子古墳 File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1号墳 File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4号墳 == 大室公園 == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|大室公園・時の広場]] {{Main|大室公園}} 現在、大室古墳群の周辺は[[公園]]として整備されており、[[日本の歴史公園100選]]に選定されている。 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 前二子古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|527|前二子古墳}}。 * 中二子古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|528|中二子古墳}}。 * 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳 - 1927年（昭和2年）4月8日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|401|529|後二子古墳ならびに小古墳}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == *前橋市教育委員会(編)　『前橋市 大室古墳群－前二子古墳・中二子古墳・後二子古墳・小二子古墳－』 == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[ヤマト王権]] * [[毛野]] * [[梅木遺跡]] == 外部リンク == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 国指定文化財等データベース 前二子古墳] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 中二子古墳] - [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 文化遺産データベース 前二子古墳] - [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 中二子古墳] - [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 後二子古墳ならびに小古墳] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html 群馬県埋蔵文化財調査事業団 大室古墳群] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html 前橋市 大室古墳群について] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html 大室公園] {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=群馬県}} {{デフォルトソート:おおむろこふんくんくんまけん}} [[Category:群馬県の古墳]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:11]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>

The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Maefutago Kofun]] → [[Mae Futago Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Ushirofutago Kofun]] → [[Ushiro Futago Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maebashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gunma Prefecture|qq=}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|ja|赤城山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Akagi|qq=}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}}, {{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}}, and {{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Mae Futago Kofun|lt=Maefutago Kofun|ja|前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maefutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Naka Futago Kofun|lt=Nakafutago Kofun|ja|中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakafutago Kofun|qq=}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushiro Futago Kofun|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun|ja|後二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushirofutago Kofun|qq=}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|ja|ヤマト王権|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Kingship|qq=}} * {{ill|Keno Province|ja|毛野|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keno Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]
