revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:群馬県の古墳
revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}

revision.text:
'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=}}</ref>

'''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."

revision.comment: Bot: categorize based on Wikidata P11250 property
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>

== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}

== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park]

These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park] {{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)

== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>

revision.comment: Bot: reorganize categories (alphabetical, deduplicated, moved to bottom)
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park] {{Kofun navbar}}

These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.

The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]]. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of [[:en:Mount Akagi|Mount Akagi]] within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] (keyhole-shaped mounds): [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]], [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]], and [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]]. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * [[:en:Maefutago Kofun|Maefutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Nakafutago Kofun|Nakafutago Kofun]] - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * [[:en:Ushirofutago Kofun|Ushirofutago Kofun]] and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * [[:en:Yamato Kingship|Yamato Kingship]] * [[:en:Keno Province|Keno Province]] * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)]][[ja:大室古墳群 (前橋市)]] {{Infobox ancient site|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q17223487]] Wikidata name: Omuro Kofun Group [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omuro Kofun Group''' (大室古墳群, Ōmuro Kofungun) is an ancient Kofun complex located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}. Four of the kofun (Kofuns) are designated as {{ill|Historic Sites of Japan|ja|国の史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Historic Sites of Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The group is situated in the southern foothills of {{ill|Mount Akagi|en|Mount Akagi|WD=Q415251|lt=Mount Akagi}} within the Jōnan area. The complex consists of three large {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} (keyhole-shaped mounds): {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}}, {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}}, and {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun}}. These mounds are believed to have been constructed sequentially from the early 6th century to the late 6th century.
== Main Kofun == * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun}} * {{ill|Shofutago Kofun|ja|小二子古墳|1=Sho Futago Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shofutago Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Sho Futago Kofun|qq=}} * Uchibori No. 1 Kofun (M-1) * Uchibori No. 4 Kofun (M-4)
Excavations have revealed additional small round tumuli near Uchibori No. 1 Kofun.
<gallery> File:Maefutago-kofun 2.jpg|Maefutago Kofun File:Nakafutago-kofun 4.jpg|Nakafutago Kofun File:Ushirofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Ushirofutago Kofun File:Shofutago-kofun 1.jpg|Shofutago Kofun File:Uchibori Kofun Group-M1, funkyu.jpg|M-1 Kofun File:M-4-kofun.jpg|M-4 Kofun </gallery>
== Omuro Park == [[File:Omuro Park Toki-no-hiroba.jpg|thumb|Toki-no-Hiroba at Omuro Park]] {{Main|Omuro Park}} The surrounding area of the Omuro Kofun Group has been developed into a park, known as Omuro Park, which is included in the "100 Historic Parks of Japan."
== Cultural Properties == === National Historic Sites === * {{ill|Maefutago Kofun|en|Maefutago Kofun|WD=Q17221412|lt=Maefutago Kofun}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527|title=前二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * {{ill|Nakafutago Kofun|en|Nakafutago Kofun|WD=Q17229473|lt=Nakafutago Kofun}} - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528|title=中二子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref> * {{ill|Ushirofutago Kofun|en|Ushirofutago Kofun|WD=Q17194124|lt=Ushirofutago Kofun}} and associated smaller mounds - Designated on April 8, 1927.<ref>{{ill|National Cultural Properties Database|ja|国指定文化財等データベース|surl=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529|title=後二子古墳ならびに小古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Cultural Properties Database|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}</ref>
== References == * Maebashi City Board of Education (Ed.), ''Maebashi Ōmuro Kofun Group - Maefutago Kofun, Nakafutago Kofun, Ushirofutago Kofun, Shofutago Kofun''. == See Also == {{Commonscat|Omuro Kofun Group (Maebashi)}} * {{ill|Yamato Kingship|en|Yamato Kingship|WD=Q2658252|lt=Yamato Kingship}} * {{ill|Keno Province|en|Keno Province|WD=Q1886428|lt=Keno Province}} * {{ill|Umeki Site|ja|梅木遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umeki Site|qq=}}
== External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/527 National Cultural Properties Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/528 National Cultural Properties Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/529 National Cultural Properties Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/190322 Cultural Heritage Database: Maefutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/204923 Cultural Heritage Database: Nakafutago Kofun] * [https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/137894 Cultural Heritage Database: Ushirofutago Kofun and smaller mounds] * [http://www.gunmaibun.org/remain/guide/tyumo/omurokohun.html Gunma Archaeological Research Association: Omuro Kofun Group] * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/bunka_sports_kanko/7/3/11373.html Maebashi City: About Omuro Kofun Group] - [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kensetsu/koenkanrijimusho/shisetsu/2/5307.html Omuro Park] {{Kofun navbar}}
